|owners=[[Rudy]]<ref name="RSI" /><ref name="Rudy'sCar">The name "Rudy's Car" refers to [[Factor 5]] employee [[Rudolf Stember]]'s personal 1969 Buick Electra convertible, which the in-game car is modeled after. As such, many sources that list the code to unlock the Buick refer to the car as "Rudy's car."</ref>

{{Quote|This classic convertible is a relic from a galaxy far, far away. Equipped with cluster missiles, this extremely heavy armored vehicle is perfect for beating the poodoo out of any Imperial forces.|General Carlist Rieekan, describing the Buick|<ref name="RSIII" />}}
The '''Buick''' was a unique vehicle called a [[Groundcar/Legends|car]] or, more specifically, a [[convertible]], that was capable of ground, [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]], and [[Space warfare/Legends|space combat]]. It was a unique design in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], coming from outside known [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. It included a very durable hull and an unseen engine system powerful enough to propel it through space at a high rate of speed. The Buick also contained hidden weaponry consisting of [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and [[Cluster missile/Legends|cluster]] [[Missile/Legends|missile]] launchers that were effective against a number of different enemies, including [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], tanks, infantry, space- and land-based structures, and even [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]].

The Buick's interior included a detailed damage indicator and map, as well as a [[steering wheel]] and several other dials and readouts. The convertible could perform in [[Planet/Legends|planetside]] combat as a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]]. Later, around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the vessel functioned at higher altitudes as an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]]. Around the same [[Time/Legends|time]] period it took on space combat as well. The inclusion of a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] further added to the Buick's strengths and allowed the ship to operate on its own against enemy targets.

At some point, the car was owned by an individual named [[Rudy]]; during this time it was simply known as '''Rudy's Car'''. By [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the Buick was in the possession of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]], who utilized it in the events following the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] and the eventual [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|recapture]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The ship was old enough to be considered a relic by 0 ABY, when the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] started using the craft in their [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and continued to use it when the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was formed, into as late as [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].

==Description==
[[File:Buick1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The front end of the Buick.]]
The Buick was a jet [[black]] [[convertible]], with [[chrome]] accent markings and trim.<ref name="RSI" /> Described in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] as a classic vessel by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]],<ref name="RSIII" /> the car's hull was also noted as being heavily [[Armor/Legends|armored]], protecting the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and any passengers. Above the pilot's head, an awning-like rooftop rested and could retract into the rear of the vessel when the Buick reached a cruising speed, and would cover the pilot again when the craft was accelerating.<ref name="RSIII" /> Two plate-like objects rested on both the front and rear of the Buick. On these white plates, letters from the [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabet]] spelled out "[[California]]" and "KOELSH."<ref name="RSI" /> These were later removed.<ref name="RSII" /><ref name="RSIII" /> Lighting devices sat on the front and rear of the vehicle.<ref name="RSIII" />

Inside the vehicle, multiple seats allowed seating for many passengers, including a spot next to the pilot. There was also a location somewhere in the vehicle for an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] to reside. This [[Droid/Legends|droid]] would handle any repairs the Buick would need during any enemy entanglements.<ref name="RSIII" /> When used as a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]], as the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] did in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] was very plain and consisted of a roofless area with a row of lights, dials, and switches located in front of the pilot.<ref name="BfN" /> The car was controlled by a dashboard-mounted [[steering wheel]]<ref name="BfN" /> and a set of pedals beneath the pilot's feet.<ref name="RSII" /> By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the car was being used in the higher [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheres]] of [[Planet/Legends|planets]] as an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] by the Rebel Alliance,<ref name="RSI" /> during which time the pilot was surrounded by multiple displays of different uses.<ref name="RSII" /> Around the same time the Buick was also capable of taking on [[Realspace/Legends|spaceflight]]. By this time, instrument panel contained few displays, including a display for a map of the surrounding region and enemies and allies within, a way to summon [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmates]] and transmit orders, a high-quality damage indicator, and weapon readouts.<ref name="RSIII" /> Also present was a [[Mirror/Legends|mirror]] hanging from the windscreen, and two mirrors on each door;<ref name="RSI" /> these allowed the pilot to view events behind him, though none offered a very clear picture.<ref name="RSIII" />

[[File:Buick5.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Buick leaves the hangar.]]
The Buick's interior included two decorative ornaments: one of the [[bunny]] [[Max (bunny)|Max]], dangling from the roof-mounted mirror, and another of an unidentified creature, whose most notable attribute was its bobbing head.<ref name="RSIII" /> Also included in the car was a [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]], allowing the pilot to easily distinguish enemies from friendly craft, as well as detailed views on mission objectives.<ref name="RSIII" />

[[File:BuickBfN1.jpg|thumb|left|280px|The convertible in [[Theed/Legends|Theed]].]]
The car's engines, while powerful enough to move the craft through space at a high rate of speed, were not outwardly visible. Rather, two exhaust ports revealed the propulsion systems of the Buick; their glow was reminiscent of other starfighters of its time.<ref name="RSI" /> The [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] engines responsible for making the car hover would often fail, especially when exiting a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] into space; when the Buick reached the end of the landing deck it would drop as it exited the starship. The [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] engines would soon initiate and allow the pilot to regain control and movement.<ref name="RSII" /><ref name="RSIII" /> Also present on the convertible were four [[wheel]]s, two located at the fore and two located at the aft. The pair toward the rear of the craft were mostly covered by the hull. The wheels were not used, as their use as a method of transportation was made unnecessary due to its hovering nature.<ref name="RSI" /> Later, the car's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] allowed it to travel to distant places without the need for additional support craft or units; in this way, it was entirely possible for the craft to act autonomously.<ref name="RSIII" />

As was the case with the engines, the weapons present on the Buick were not outwardly visible. There was a large amount of firepower, however, in the form of dual [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and two [[Cluster missile/Legends|cluster missile]] launchers.<ref name="RSII" /> The laser cannons were able to provide a steady, long-lasting stream of fire. While the lasers fired from the front of the car, the cluster missile launchers were located toward the rear and underneath the convertible;<ref name="RSIII" /> each launcher contained a payload of three.<ref name="RSI" />

==History==
The Buick was once under the ownership of [[Rudy]], and was known at that time simply as "Rudy's Car."<ref name="Rudy'sCar" /> In 32 BBY, the car turned up in the hands of the [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] Security Forces, where it participated in ground-based missions surrounding the events of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. Although allied with the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]], the craft was piloted by a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]].<ref name="BfN" />

Later, some time as early as 0 ABY, the car came into the Rebel Alliance's possession; at this time it was old enough to be considered a relic. The Rebellion stored it in the hangars of its [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]]; Rebel pilots, including [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]],<ref name="RSIII" /> could use it during missions against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="RSI" /><ref name="RSII" /><ref name="RSIII" /> The convertible remained in their possession, and later that of the successor [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], throughout the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="RSIII" /> as late as the [[Battle of Calamari|Battle of]] [[Dac/Legends|Calamari]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="RSI" />

Rebel Alliance General Carlist Rieekan mentioned that the car was "a relic from a [[Extragalactic/Legends|galaxy far, far away]]," when covering the origins and specs of the vehicle.<ref name="RSIII" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BuickCompare.jpg|thumb|left|280px|[[Rudolf Stember]]'s own 1969 [[Wikipedia:Buick Electra|Buick Electra]] convertible, compared to the in-game version.]]
The Buick is a recurring [[Easter egg (virtual)|easter egg]] present throughout every game in the ''Rogue Squadron'' series of games, as well as ''[[Star Wars: Episode I: Battle for Naboo]]'', as a special unlockable. In ''Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader'' and ''Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike'', it replaced the [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] once unlocked. The only way to unlock the convertible was through the use of [[Wikipedia:cheat codes|cheat codes]]. However, the Buick is absent from the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] version, as the cheat code to unlock it does not work properly.<ref name="RSI-PC">[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] version}}</ref> The source files do remain in the PC version, and it is possible to unlock the craft using unofficial game modifications.<ref name="RSI-PC" /><ref name="RS1-mod">{{WebCite|url=http://rogue.squadron.tripod.com/roguecar.htm|text=CHAPTER II THE HIDDEN VEHICLE|work=The Secrets of Rogue Squadron|archivedate=20010131205500}}</ref> In ''Star Wars: Battle for Naboo'', the ship is accessible in both versions of the game without any modification.<ref name="BfN" />

In ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', the Buick contains two references to other [[LucasArts]] games: first, a swinging [[Max (bunny)|Max]] head, from the classic adventure series [[Wikipedia:Sam and Max|''Sam and Max'']], and second, a bobblehead monkey, reminiscent of the adventure series [[Wikipedia:Monkey Island|''Monkey Island'']].<ref name="RSIII" />

The vehicle is based on American automobiles from the [[1960s]], possibly as a tribute to [[George Lucas]]'s proclaimed appreciation of classic automotive vehicles.<ref name="AmericanGraffiti">[[Wikipedia:American Graffiti|''American Graffiti'']]</ref> The in-game car is modeled after [[Factor 5]] employee [[Rudolf Stember]]'s personal 1969 [[Wikipedia:Buick Electra|Buick Electra]] convertible.<ref name="StemberSite">{{WebCite|url=http://www.stember.com/stember/car.shtml|text=1969 Buick Electra convertible|work=stember.com|archivedate=20050311224006}}</ref> Due to this, the designation "Rudy's Car" was often used in several sources to find the cheat code to unlock the Buick in each respective game.<ref name="Rudy'sCar" />
